diseaseName: Sindrome do intestino irritavel com diarreia (SII-D)

prevalence.source: FBG and Rome IV epidemiology syntheses

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant QOL and work impairment

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rome IV criteria for IBS-D; exclude alarm features and inflammatory bowel disease

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; improves with dietary and pharmacologic therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care and gastroenterology; limited access to dietitians
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dietary modification (low FODMAP where available), antispasmodics, loperamide, rifaximin for selected cases, CBT when available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited dietitian access and underdiagnosis due to stigma
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Basic medications available in SUS and growing awareness
